title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems at Samsung.com and Kolon is a near-perfect domain match for Airbnb's R&P team — the core skills (search relevance, behavioral signals, A/B experimentation, ML partnership, query understanding) are all directly evidenced. The 9-year experience baseline is technically one year short of the stated 10+ requirement, and the role signals a slightly higher scope (multi-surface marketplace, multi-objective balancing including host fairness and marketplace health) than Riley's current IC3-level work, placing this at a mild stretch. Sponsorship is unconfirmed, which introduces meaningful risk at a company of Airbnb's size where sponsorship is not always guaranteed.
recommendation_reason: Domain, toolset, and PM archetype are exceptionally well-matched; Riley's search ranking, personalization, and conversational AI work at Samsung directly mirrors Airbnb R&P's charter.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered search, personalization, and recommendation systems at global scale, including hybrid search launches serving 10M+ monthly users and ML ranking improvements delivering 30%+ precision gains. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to define relevance strategy, design rigorous A/B experiments, and ship systems that measurably improve discovery, engagement, and conversion across the full customer journey.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement is a soft gap (Riley has 9 years) — address this by emphasizing the depth and complexity of ML systems owned, not just tenure. The marketplace health / host-side fairness dimension is new territory; prepare a brief POV on two-sided marketplace ranking tradeoffs (e.g., exposure fairness for hosts vs. guest relevance) to demonstrate readiness for Airbnb's unique dual-stakeholder model.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience directly in AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and e-commerce conversion funnels maps almost perfectly onto Airbnb's Guest Discovery & Homepage mandate — including hybrid search, recommendation APIs, behavioral ranking signals, and A/B testing at scale (10M+ monthly users). The AEO pipeline, conversational AI chat, and Samsung.com homepage-style discovery roadmap are particularly strong proof points. The one gap is consumer marketplace experience (travel/hospitality) and the marketing narrative/GTM ownership the JD emphasizes, which Riley's resume covers lightly.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain alignment — AI discovery, personalization, search ranking, and consumer UX at scale — with metrics that will stand out to Airbnb's hiring bar.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years leading search, discovery, and personalization experiences for large-scale consumer platforms — including AI-powered homepage search, recommendation APIs, and conversational discovery for 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record of shipping relevance-driven discovery systems with measurable business outcomes (CTR +20–45%, AOV +25%, NPS +20%) in partnership with ML, design, and cross-functional stakeholders.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The JD places notable emphasis on product marketing narrative, external communications, and lifecycle/growth marketing integration — areas Riley's resume mentions but doesn't lead with. Address this directly in the cover letter by citing the AEO pipeline launch (GTM execution, cross-functional coordination with marketing) and the conversational AI chat launch (marketing, UX, and engineering co-ownership). If Riley has any experience presenting to executives or crafting product narratives for press/media, add a brief bullet or cover letter line to close this gap.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley is a strong match on the core PM mechanics: 9 years of consumer product experience, a deep track record in e-commerce discovery, personalization/recommendation systems, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional delivery at scale — all of which directly map to Instacart's minimum qualifications. The domain is adjacent (grocery shopping experience vs. Riley's Samsung.com and Kolonmall e-commerce work), and the preferred qualifications around e-commerce, recommendations, and search are a near-perfect overlay. The one meaningful gap is the Health/nutrition/wellness vertical, which is the specific product area this role owns — Riley has no explicit experience there, but the role signals it as "nice to have" rather than required, and the zero-to-one consumer discovery framing is very much in Riley's wheelhouse.
recommendation_reason: Strong consumer e-commerce, discovery, personalization, and experimentation background maps tightly to core requirements; health domain gap is a preferred — not required — qualification.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery, personalization, and shopping experiences for millions of consumers across Samsung.com, Amazon, and e-commerce platforms. Proven track record of shipping zero-to-one consumer products — from hybrid search and recommendation systems to conversational AI — with measurable impact on engagement, conversion, and retention at scale.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing — directly mirrors Instacart's personalization and conversion mandate.

Defined on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users — demonstrates scaled consumer discovery ownership.
Launched ML-powered virtual try-on with size recommendation, achieving 70% adoption and 40% reduction in return rate MoM — showcases zero-to-one consumer product execution from discovery through GTM.
gap_handling: The Health/nutrition/wellness vertical is listed as a preferred — not required — qualification. In your cover letter and resume summary, draw an explicit bridge: frame your work on dietary/preference-aware product discovery, personalization filtering (e.g., category-specific finder experiences, taxonomy optimization), and consumer trust-building as adjacent foundations for health-forward features. If you have any personal passion for health, nutrition, or wellness, briefly surface it authentically — PMs owning zero-to-one bets are often evaluated more on problem-solving acumen and consumer empathy than domain-specific credentials.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's core experience — AI-powered hybrid search, search ranking, relevance metrics, and behavioral signal optimization at Samsung.com for 10M+ monthly users — maps directly to Faire's Search Algorithms role. The domain (search discovery in a marketplace) and PM archetype (algorithmic/AI search PM) are nearly identical. However, the role is titled Staff PM, which is a stretch above Riley's target seniority, and Faire's marketplace is B2B wholesale between retailers and brands — a slightly different context than B2C e-commerce, though Riley's Amazon B2B seller tools experience provides meaningful bridging evidence.
recommendation_reason: Strong search/AI domain alignment and directly relevant evidence, but the Staff PM title is a stretch and sponsorship is unconfirmed for an H1B candidate.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale, including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and relevance metric frameworks for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to ship measurable improvements — precision@3 +30%, CTR +45% — in marketplace and e-commerce environments, with additional B2B platform experience from Amazon.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The Staff PM title typically implies a level above Senior PM with stronger scope/influence signals. To close this gap, Riley should explicitly frame cross-org influence, strategic vision ownership, and any examples of setting direction for multiple teams or a full search platform — not just individual features. The B2B wholesale context at Faire is also different from B2C; Riley should lean into the Amazon B2B seller tools experience and reframe Samsung marketplace work in terms of multi-sided marketplace dynamics wherever possible.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and deep search, discovery, and e-commerce growth experience — all of which signal genuine AI passion and analytical rigor that Anthropic values. However, the role specifically requires majority-of-career growth PM experience on API or developer platform products, which is a meaningful gap; Riley's work has been consumer/B2C growth and B2B seller tooling, not developer-facing API platforms or usage-based monetization funnels. The Amazon B2B listing tool is the closest proxy to developer tooling but is not a true developer platform. Seniority (6+ years, $305K–$385K band) is a strong target-level match at 9 years experience.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/growth fundamentals and seniority match, but the core requirement of developer platform / API growth PM experience is a clear gap that needs deliberate framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through AI-powered search, personalization, and discovery systems — including launching products used by 10M+ monthly users and 2M+ B2B sellers at Samsung and Amazon. Passionate about developer-facing AI products and experienced translating complex ML capabilities into high-impact, data-driven growth strategies across acquisition, activation, and monetization funnels.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating technical platform PM experience at scale

Defined success metrics and led instrumentation strategy for search experiences, leveraging behavioral insights to increase search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ — evidence of rigorous funnel analytics and KPI ownership

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — showing ability to ship AI-native growth features rapidly
gap_handling: Directly address the developer platform gap in your cover letter: frame the Amazon B2B seller platform as a technical, self-serve, usage-based product (it is), and highlight your AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as evidence of building for technical/API-consuming audiences. Consider noting any personal use of Claude API or side projects with LLM APIs to signal authentic developer empathy.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley has strong direct experience in AI-powered personalization, search/discovery, and LLM-adjacent systems — all of which map well to Airbnb's AI Personalization platform mission. The conversational AI, recommendation API, and hybrid search work at Samsung directly demonstrate consumer-facing AI personalization at scale. However, this is a Staff PM role (one level above Riley's target Senior PM), it leans heavily into platform/infrastructure ownership (RAG, contextual retrieval architectures, prompt optimization at infra level) rather than Riley's predominantly consumer-product and e-commerce personalization background, and there is no explicit customer support / CS domain experience — a notable gap given the role sits within CS Platform.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI personalization and consumer-facing product match, but Staff-level seniority stretch and CS Platform domain gap make this a competitive application requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years building consumer-facing personalization, AI search, and LLM-driven experiences at scale — including a recommendation platform on a $2B+ GMV platform and a conversational AI product serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record partnering with ML and data science to define retrieval strategies, personalization signals, and experiment frameworks that drive measurable impact across complex AI systems.
top_bullets: Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The CS/support domain is new territory — briefly address this in your cover letter by framing personalization as domain-agnostic: the signals, retrieval architecture, and LLM context engineering are the same whether the user is shopping or resolving a support ticket. Highlight your conversational AI work as the closest proxy to an AI support agent experience. On seniority, lean into your cross-functional leadership breadth (Samsung: 10M+ users, 3 international markets; Amazon: 2M+ sellers) to signal Staff-level readiness without overstating your title history.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and e-commerce growth — partially transfer to this role, particularly around AI-powered self-service and the "discovery-led" redesign of the Help Center. However, the role is fundamentally about customer support infrastructure, help center/knowledge base platforms, and CustOps tooling — a domain Riley has no direct resume evidence for. The growth context and AI-chat experience (conversational AI at Samsung) are genuine overlaps, but the support enablement and internal agent tooling dimensions represent a real gap.
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/discovery overlap and growth DNA, but no direct help center, support enablement, or CustOps tooling experience — requires meaningful tailoring and a clear narrative pivot.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered discovery and conversational experiences at scale, including a production conversational AI chat for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and growth-focused personalization systems that drove measurable trial and retention lift. Proven at leading cross-functional teams through complex, blue-sky redesigns — from scope definition through iterative delivery — with a track record of turning discovery surfaces into quantifiable growth levers.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15% via segmentation-driven CRM marketing
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit help center, knowledge base, or CustOps tooling experience on the resume. To close this gap, the cover letter and resume summary should explicitly connect the Samsung conversational AI chat (product discovery + support deflection use case) and the AEO pipeline (structured content surfacing) to help center strategy. Framing the Samsung chat as a "self-service support deflection" tool — rather than purely a discovery feature — is credible and directly maps to the role's deflection rate KPI.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience, SQL/Tableau tooling, and track record of driving measurable outcomes at scale are genuine strengths that map to this role. However, the role is explicitly a Staff PM focused on an internal AI Platform (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, platform-as-a-product for internal teams) — a platform/infra PM archetype that differs meaningfully from Riley's consumer-facing search and discovery background. The specific requirements around agentic AI, multi-agent orchestration, build-vs-buy SaaS decisions, and CXO-level platform evangelism are areas where Riley's resume has thin or indirect evidence.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML credentials and tooling overlap, but the Staff-level platform/infra PM archetype and agentic AI focus are a meaningful stretch from Riley's consumer search and discovery core.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — from LLM-driven pipelines and hybrid search to conversational AI and recommendation engines — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers and data scientists to define platform strategy, instrument KPIs, and drive measurable adoption across cross-functional teams of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform PM experience: this role is fundamentally about building and evangelizing an internal AI platform for other teams (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, SaaS vendor decisions), not shipping consumer-facing features. Riley should lean into the AEO pipeline and conversational AI bullets as the closest proxies to platform thinking, and explicitly call out any experience with internal tooling, API design, or serving internal stakeholders as customers. If applying, Riley should be prepared to speak concretely about agentic AI frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, etc.) and multi-agent patterns in the cover letter or screen.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong AI/Search PM, but this role is explicitly a Growth/Monetization PM role — requiring deep ownership of pricing, packaging, subscription funnels, and free-to-paid conversion, which are only tangentially represented in Riley's resume. The Kolon growth work (CRM, retention, first-purchase funnel) and Amazon cost-savings bullets show some monetization sensibility, but none of Riley's roles are primarily Growth PM roles at a subscription-scale business. The AI domain alignment and Anthropic's prestige are strong draws, but the archetype mismatch (Search/Discovery vs. Monetization/Growth) is the central risk.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain fit and brand upside, but Riley's core archetype (AI Search/Discovery) is misaligned with the Monetization/Growth PM role; apply only with a tailored narrative around growth and conversion work.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth and AI-powered product experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon, with a track record of measurable revenue impact across e-commerce and discovery funnels. Experienced in A/B testing, funnel optimization, and cross-functional execution at scale — now focused on applying AI product expertise to drive monetization and growth at a mission-driven AI company.
top_bullets: Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley has never held a role with explicit ownership of pricing, packaging, or subscription upgrade funnels — the core asks of this role. To bridge this gap, the resume and cover letter should front-load the Kolon growth-era work (first-purchase rate +30%, retention +7%, re-purchase +15%, AOV +25%) and explicitly frame them as monetization outcomes. Riley should also be prepared in interviews to speak to how discovery and conversion funnel work directly connects to revenue — and ideally articulate a point of view on Claude's pricing and packaging strategy.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience, SQL skills, LLM familiarity, and strong track record with data-intensive systems are genuinely relevant — and the AI search + pipeline work at Samsung is compelling. However, the role is fundamentally B2B SaaS (observability/analytics platform), and Riley's background skews heavily toward consumer e-commerce and retail search. More critically, the role requires deep domain knowledge of BI tooling, semantic layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack (dbt, Snowflake, Looker, etc.) — areas not evidenced anywhere in the resume. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial build, governance layer design, and BI-competitive landscape research are also outside Riley's demonstrated scope.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product and SQL credentials are a partial match, but missing B2B SaaS depth and BI/data stack domain knowledge create meaningful gaps for a 0-to-1 product at this level.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving LLM-powered data products, search ranking systems, and structured data pipelines at scale — including conversational AI, AEO metadata pipelines, and multi-modal ML workflows at Samsung and Amazon. Experienced shipping 0-to-1 AI experiences that serve both technical and non-technical users, with a strong foundation in SQL, experimentation, and cross-functional product delivery in data-intensive environments.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories
gap_handling: Directly acknowledge the BI/data stack gap in a cover letter by framing your AEO metadata pipeline and structured catalog work as analogous to semantic layer design — you've built systems that normalize and govern structured data for AI consumption at scale. Mention any personal exposure to tools like dbt, Snowflake, or Looker even if exploratory. The "AI-pilled" signal is important — reference specific AI tools you use daily (Cursor, Claude, etc.) to show cultural fit with what they're looking for.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, search ranking, recommendation systems — that directly map to Okta's "AI-native experiences" mandate. However, this is a Principal PM role requiring 10+ years, and Riley has 9 years, creating a slight seniority mismatch; more critically, the domain is enterprise identity/IAM and internal productivity tooling (IT, HR, Finance, GTM workflows), which is meaningfully different from Riley's consumer e-commerce and search expertise. The "agentic systems," "enterprise compliance," and "responsible AI" nice-to-haves are also largely absent from Riley's background, and the role is clearly a stretch above Riley's target seniority band (Senior PM / PM III).
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/ML product evidence makes Riley competitive, but the enterprise identity domain and Principal-level seniority bar are notable gaps requiring deliberate tailoring.
